## Plot <code>seroincidence.by</code> log-likelihoods {.unnumbered}


### Description

Plots log-likelihood curves by stratum, for <code>seroincidence.by</code> objects



### Usage

<pre><code class='language-R'>## S3 method for class 'seroincidence.by'
autoplot(object, ncol = min(3, length(object)), ...)
</code></pre>


### Arguments

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="object">object</code></td>
<td>
a '&quot;seroincidence.by&quot;' object (from <code>est_seroincidence_by()</code>)
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="ncol">ncol</code></td>
<td>
number of columns to use for panel of plots
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="...">...</code></td>
<td>

Arguments passed on to <code>autoplot.seroincidence</code>


<dl>
<dt><code>log_x</code></dt><dd>should the x-axis be on a logarithmic scale (<code>TRUE</code>)
or linear scale (<code>FALSE</code>, default)?
</dd>
</dl>
</td></tr>
</table>


### Value

a <code>"patchwork"</code> object: a single or <code>list()</code> of <code>ggplot2::ggplot()</code>s



### Examples
```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, eval=TRUE}
library("serocalculator")


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

xs_data <-
  sees_pop_data_pk_100

curve <-
  typhoid_curves_nostrat_100 %>%
  filter(antigen_iso %in% c("HlyE_IgA", "HlyE_IgG"))

noise <-
  example_noise_params_pk

est2 <- est_seroincidence_by(
  strata = c("catchment"),
  pop_data = xs_data,
  sr_params = curve,
  curve_strata_varnames= NULL,
  noise_strata_varnames = NULL,
  noise_params = noise,
  antigen_isos = c("HlyE_IgG", "HlyE_IgA"),
  #num_cores = 8, #Allow for parallel processing to decrease run time
  build_graph = TRUE
)

# Plot the log-likelihood curve
autoplot(est2)



```
